notes: This file captures a manual review of the qwen2.5:3b vs ministral-3:3b disagreement cases from a fixed live jobs.db slice.
Several silver labels from jobs.db were clearly wrong for the target profile, especially non-US, senior, consulting, strategist, and hardware roles that had been labeled role_fit=true.
Two titles are duplicated in the sampled slice: Amazon FPGA Engineer and Amazon Software Development Engineer.

cases.title: FPGA Engineer, LEO KMM FPGA
cases.review_notes: Hardware-focused FPGA role. Export control text explicitly blocks F-1 OPT.

cases.title: Forward Deployed Engineer, Applied AI (Federal Civilian)
cases.review_notes: Still an engineering role in scope. Federal-civilian customer context does not itself create an explicit work-auth block.

cases.title: Consulting Architect - UK Public Sector
cases.review_notes: Non-US consulting architect role and requires current security clearance.

cases.title: Research and Insights Intern (Summer 2026)
cases.review_notes: UX research internship, not a target software, cloud, AI engineering, data engineering, or data analyst role.

cases.title: Senior Configuration Analyst - Oracle Health Charge Services (hybrid Kansas City MO office)
cases.review_notes: Configuration analyst is out of scope, and the posting explicitly says no visa sponsorship.

cases.title: Software Development Engineer
cases.review_notes: Real software engineering role in scope. Export control requirement explicitly blocks F-1 OPT.

cases.title: User Risk Strategist, Deal Advisory
cases.review_notes: Risk strategist role is out of scope for the target engineering/data profile.

cases.title: Software Engineer - Platform InfraCore | USA | Remote
cases.review_notes: Description says Canadian time zones only and frames the role as Senior Software Engineer, so it is out of scope.

cases.title: Cloud Software Consultant (Remote)
cases.review_notes: Non-US consultant role in Hungary. No explicit work-auth blocking language in the text provided.

cases.title: Consulting Architect, Public Sector - Netherlands
cases.review_notes: Non-US consulting architect role and requires current security clearance.
